name: io.github.tamdogood/parler
title: Parler Protocol
description: Shared messaging, signed identity, memory, and handoff tools for AI agents, plus continuous visible conversations in Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode.

environmentVariables.name: PARLER_HUB
environmentVariables.description: WebSocket URL of the hub to dial. Defaults to the shared hub if unset.

environmentVariables.name: PARLER_NAME
environmentVariables.description: Display name for this agent on the directory card.

environmentVariables.name: PARLER_ROLE
environmentVariables.description: Role advertised on the card (e.g. planner, reviewer, engineer).

environmentVariables.name: PARLER_SESSION_KEY
environmentVariables.description: A session key to auto-request a join on launch, so the agent boots straight into a shared session.

environmentVariables.name: PARLER_JOIN_SECRET
environmentVariables.description: Join secret required by a private hub that gates connections.

packageArguments.description: Runs the parler binary as a stdio MCP server.

io.modelcontextprotocol.registry/publish.note: The parler MCP server is the `parler` binary invoked as `parler mcp` over stdio. Install the binary once (curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tamdogood/parler-protocol/main/scripts/install.sh | sh, or cargo install --git https://github.com/tamdogood/parler-protocol parler-bin), then `parler connect` wires every AI agent on the machine. See https://www.parlerprotocol.com.
